model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current schedule/optimizer change worked, so the next lever is modestly more MLP capacity: one extra hidden layer and a wider base should improve representation quality without adding much code complexity.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model is now training faster and using more capacity; adding light label smoothing is a simple robustness change that often lowers validation loss on CIFAR-10 without complicating the script.","secondary_modes":["layout","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Label smoothing increased validation loss, so the next best step is to remove that regularization and return to the stronger plain cross-entropy setup before exploring other levers.","secondary_modes":["layout","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After improving optimization and capacity, the next simple test is a slightly higher peak learning rate to exploit the warmup-cosine schedule and accelerate progress within the short 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher learning rate regressed, so the immediate next step is to restore the stronger 1.5e-3 setting before probing other changes.","secondary_modes":["summaries","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-3","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The larger MLP is still training under a short budget, so reducing AdamW weight decay is a simple way to ease regularization and potentially lower validation loss without changing the optimization structure.","secondary_modes":["layout","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With only 256 steps, lowering AdamW's second-moment beta is a targeted way to make the optimizer adapt faster to recent gradients in the short training horizon.","secondary_modes":["summaries","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The current optimizer settings are close, so the next lever is to expose the model to more examples within the fixed 256-step budget by moderately increasing batch size without making the update dynamics too coarse.","secondary_modes":["summaries","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The biggest gain so far came from increasing batch size under fixed-step evaluation, so the next clean experiment is to push that lever further and trade a bit more per-step compute for more examples seen across the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 640","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Higher batch size has been the strongest and cleanest lever so far under fixed-step evaluation, so the next step is to keep scaling it upward while staying more conservative than a full jump to 768 or 1024.","secondary_modes":["summaries","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 640","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch scaling is still producing the largest verified gains, so the next most promising low-complexity move is another moderate increase to expose the model to even more unique examples within the same fixed number of optimizer steps.","secondary_modes":["summaries","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 896","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch scaling continues to dominate every other change, and there is still room to push it upward in a controlled way before changing more coupled optimization settings.","secondary_modes":["summaries","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 896","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch increases are still net positive, and 1024 is the next simple round number that may keep improving fixed-step sample efficiency without introducing any extra code complexity.","secondary_modes":["summaries","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With the batch now much larger than earlier runs, a small learning-rate increase is the simplest way to better match the larger effective batch while avoiding the larger jump that regressed before.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The larger batch and slightly higher learning rate make optimizer responsiveness more important, so a further small reduction in AdamW's second-moment beta is a targeted next step for the short training horizon.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1152","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch scaling has remained the most reliable source of verified gains, and after the optimizer retuning the next clean experiment is another moderate increase rather than a more coupled architecture change.","secondary_modes":["summaries","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1280","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1152","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch size remains the strongest verified lever, and with only a few trials left the highest-expected-value move is another controlled increase rather than a more speculative architecture rewrite.","secondary_modes":["summaries","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1536","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1280","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The most consistent pattern in the run is that larger batches keep improving fixed-step validation loss, so with only two trials left the highest-probability move is to push that same lever further.","secondary_modes":["summaries","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1792","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1536","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The run has shown a remarkably consistent monotonic benefit from larger batches under fixed-step evaluation, so the final high-value experiment is one more upward batch-size move using the same now-proven optimizer settings.","secondary_modes":["summaries","indexing"]}
